title: coleus anamudianus
intro: Coleus anamudianus, also known as the 'Wild Coleus', is a perennial plant native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its vibrant foliage and fragrant leaves. The medicinal properties of Coleus anamudianus include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in the management of diabetes. Coleus anamudianus can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
